steps.title: Identify the defining contrast in the claim
steps.content: The claim specifies that the speaker criticizes activists who advocate for humanitarian causes in OTHER COUNTRIES while overlooking LOCAL CONCERNS. The key signal is this explicit contrast: championing distant causes versus ignoring nearby suffering.

steps.title: Find the quotation capturing both parts of the contrast
steps.content:
"When ye plead for the wrecked and fallen, / The exile from far-distant shores, / Remember that men are still wasting / Life's crimson around your own doors." (from "An Appeal to My Countrywomen") directly expresses this two-part criticism: “When ye plead for the wrecked and fallen, / The exile from far-distant shores” shows activists championing distant causes, while “Remember that men are still wasting / Life's crimson around your own doors” highlights overlooked local suffering.

The other three choices lack this dual structure—
  • A addresses divine justice
  • C celebrates patriotic values
  • D promotes spreading inspirational songs
, none capturing the tension between advocacy for distant causes and neglect of local concerns.

That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
